annotations.label: Graph drawing / network diagram

anchor.snippet: A drawing of a graph or network diagram is a pictorial representation

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes abstract graphs (SimpleGraph) but has no notion of a pictorial drawing/visual representation of a graph.

annotations.label: Node–link diagram

anchor.snippet: Graphs are frequently drawn as node–link diagrams

annotations.note: A node–link diagram is a drawing convention with no counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pseudo-Lull diagrams of complete graphs

anchor.snippet: Pseudo-Lull drew diagrams of this type for complete graphs

annotations.note: Complete graphs exist in Mathlib but this historical diagrammatic convention is not formalized.

annotations.label: Arrowhead convention for directed graphs

anchor.snippet: arrowheads form a commonly used graphical convention to show their orientation

annotations.note: This is a drawing convention for depicting edge orientation, not a mathematical object in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Upward planar drawing

anchor.snippet: Upward planar drawing uses the convention that every edge is oriented from a lower vertex to a higher vertex

annotations.note: Mathlib has no planar-drawing or upward-planarity formalization.

annotations.label: Alternative adjacency representations

anchor.snippet: Alternative conventions to node–link diagrams include adjacency representations such as circle packings

annotations.note: Circle-packing and other adjacency-drawing representations are absent from Mathlib (no circlePacking found).

annotations.label: Crossing number

anchor.snippet: The crossing number of a drawing is the number of pairs of edges that cross each other

annotations.note: A grep for crossing number found no graph-drawing crossing-number definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Planar drawing as graph embedding

anchor.snippet: a graph drawing represents a graph embedding

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of planar graphs or topological graph embeddings.

annotations.label: Area of a drawing

anchor.snippet: The area of a drawing is the size of its smallest bounding box

annotations.note: Bounding-box area of a drawing is a visualization metric not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetry display

anchor.snippet: Symmetry display is the problem of finding symmetry groups within a given graph

annotations.note: Graph isomorphisms (`G ≃g G`, i.e. automorphisms) are formalized as the related symmetry infrastructure, but the drawing/display problem itself is not.

annotations.label: Edge complexity (bends)

anchor.snippet: the complexity of an edge may be measured by its number of bends

annotations.note: Edge bends are a property of a drawing and have no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Edge length measures

anchor.snippet: it is generally desirable to minimize the total length of the edges

annotations.note: Geometric edge-length measures of a drawing are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angular resolution

anchor.snippet: Angular resolution is a measure of the sharpest angles in a graph drawing

annotations.note: Angular resolution of a drawing is a visualization metric absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angular resolution bound by degree

anchor.snippet: the angular resolution can be bounded below by a function of the degree

annotations.note: No drawing angular-resolution bounds are formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Slope number

anchor.snippet: The slope number of a graph is the minimum number of distinct edge slopes needed in a drawing

annotations.note: Slope number is a drawing invariant with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Slope number of cubic and high-degree graphs

anchor.snippet: Cubic graphs have slope number at most four

annotations.note: This slope-number bound for cubic graphs is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Force-based layout

anchor.snippet: In force-based layout systems, the graph drawing software modifies an initial vertex placement

annotations.note: Force-directed layout is a drawing algorithm not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spectral layout

anchor.snippet: Spectral layout methods use as coordinates the eigenvectors of a matrix

annotations.note: The graph Laplacian matrix (whose eigenvectors spectral layout uses) is formalized, but the layout method placing vertices by eigenvector coordinates is not.

annotations.label: Orthogonal layout

anchor.snippet: Orthogonal layout methods, which allow the edges of the graph to run horizontally or vertically

annotations.note: Orthogonal drawing layout is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tree layout

anchor.snippet: Tree layout algorithms these show a rooted tree

annotations.note: Trees are formalized (SimpleGraph.IsTree) but the layout/drawing algorithm for rooted trees is not.

annotations.label: Balloon layout

anchor.snippet: the children of each node in the tree are drawn on a circle surrounding the node

annotations.note: Balloon tree layout is a drawing technique not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Layered (Sugiyama-style) drawing

anchor.snippet: Layered graph drawing methods (often called Sugiyama-style drawing) are best suited for directed acyclic graphs

annotations.note: Sugiyama-style layered drawing is not formalized in Mathlib (no Sugiyama found).

annotations.label: Arc diagram

anchor.snippet: place vertices on a line

annotations.note: Arc-diagram layout is a drawing convention absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circular layout

anchor.snippet: Circular layout methods place the vertices of the graph on a circle

annotations.note: Circular drawing layout is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dominance drawing

anchor.snippet: Dominance drawing places vertices in such a way that one vertex is upwards

annotations.note: Dominance drawing is a layout method with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Sociograms

anchor.snippet: drawings of a social network

annotations.note: Sociograms are social-network drawings not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hasse diagrams

anchor.snippet: a type of graph drawing specialized to partial orders

annotations.note: The covering relation `CovBy` (⋖) that a Hasse diagram depicts is formalized, but the diagram/drawing itself is not.

annotations.label: Dessin d'enfants

anchor.snippet: a type of graph drawing used in algebraic geometry

annotations.note: Grothendieck's dessins d'enfants are not formalized in Mathlib (no dessin found).

annotations.label: State diagrams

anchor.snippet: graphical representations of finite-state machines

annotations.note: Finite-state machines are formalized as `DFA`/`NFA`, but the state-diagram graphical representation is not.

annotations.label: Computer network diagrams

anchor.snippet: depictions of the nodes and connections in a computer network

annotations.note: Computer network diagrams are an application drawing not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flowcharts

anchor.snippet: the nodes represent the steps of an algorithm and the edges represent control flow between steps

annotations.note: Flowcharts are an applied diagram type with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Project network

anchor.snippet: graphical depiction of the chronological order in which activities of a project are to be completed

annotations.note: Project/activity network diagrams are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Data-flow diagrams

anchor.snippet: the nodes represent the components of an information system

annotations.note: Data-flow diagrams are an applied drawing type absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bioinformatics graphs

anchor.snippet: including phylogenetic trees

annotations.note: Phylogenetic trees and other bioinformatics graph drawings are not formalized in Mathlib (no phylogen found).

annotations.label: Reingold-Tilford algorithm

anchor.snippet: The Reingold-Tilford algorithm for tree drawing

annotations.note: The Reingold–Tilford tree-drawing algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kant's algorithm

anchor.snippet: which constructs a polyline drawing of a 3-connected planar graph

annotations.note: Kant's polyline drawing algorithm for planar graphs is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tamassia's bend-minimization algorithm

anchor.snippet: Tamassia's algorithm for minimizing the number of bends in an orthogonal representation of a planar graph

annotations.note: Tamassia's bend-minimization algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib (no Tamassia found).

annotations.label: Magnetic Spring Model

anchor.snippet: The Magnetic Spring Model by Sugiyama and Misue

annotations.note: The Magnetic Spring Model layout algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib.
